words.def: a small, narrow river
to become more powerful or effective; to make somebody/something more powerful or effective
words.example: Her position in the party has strengthened in recent weeks.

words.def: an area of land or water, especially a long one
words.example: a particularly dangerous stretch of road

words.def: that must be obeyed exactly
with a lot of control and rules that must be obeyed
words.example: She was brought up very strictly.

words.def: a period of time when an organized group of employees of a company stops working because of an argument over pay or conditions
an act of hitting a ball, for example with a bat or racket
the way in which the parts of something are connected together, arranged or organized; a particular arrangement of parts
a hard fight in which people try to obtain or achieve something, especially something that somebody else does not want them to have
words.example: Marx wrote about the class struggle.

words.def: used to refer to a substance, material, group of objects, etc. when you do not know the name, when the name is not important or when it is obvious what you are talking about
words.example: What's all that sticky stuff on the carpet?

words.def: extremely attractive or impressive
likely to be affected by something, especially something bad
words.example: At this stage these are proposals and are still subject to change.

words.def: to give a document, proposal, etc. to somebody in authority so that they can study or consider it
words.example: submit something, to submit an application/a claim/a proposal

words.def: happening or coming after something else
afterwards; later; after something else has happened
words.example: The original interview notes were subsequently lost.

words.def: a type of solid, liquid or gas that has particular qualities
an area where people live that is outside the centre of a city
physical or mental pain
words.example: Death finally brought an end to her suffering.

words.def: enough for a particular purpose; as much as you need
words.example: Allow sufficient time to get there.

words.def: enough for a particular purpose; as much as you need
words.example: The following day she felt sufficiently well to go to work.

words.def: an amount of money
words.example: You will be fined the sum of £200.

words.def: extremely good
a doctor who is trained to perform surgery (= medical operations that involve cutting open a person’s body)
medical treatment of injuries or diseases that involves cutting open a person’s body and often removing or replacing some parts; the branch of medicine connected with this treatment
to be all around something/somebody
words.example: surround something/somebody, Tall trees surround the lake.

words.def: that is near or around something
an investigation of the opinions, behaviour, etc. of a particular group of people, which is usually done by asking them questions
words.example: A recent survey showed 75 per cent of those questioned were in favour of the plan.

words.def: the state of continuing to live or exist, often despite difficulty or danger
a person who continues to live, especially despite being nearly killed or experiencing great danger or difficulty
words.example: the sole/only survivor of the massacre

words.def: a person who is suspected of a crime or of having done something wrong
to hang something from something else
words.example: be suspended from something, A lamp was suspended from the ceiling.

words.def: involving the use of natural products and energy in a way that does not harm the environment
to make food, drink, etc. go down your throat into your stomach
words.example: swallow (something), Always chew food well before swallowing it.

words.def: to use rude or offensive language, usually because you are angry
words.example: She fell over and swore loudly.

words.def: to clean a room, surface, etc. using a broom (= a type of brush on a long handle)
a small device that you press or move up and down in order to turn a light or piece of electrical equipment on and off
kind to somebody who is hurt or sad; showing that you understand and care about their problems
the feeling of being sorry for somebody; showing that you understand and care about somebody’s problems
words.example: sympathy for somebody, to express/feel sympathy for somebody

words.def: a small piece of paper, cloth, plastic, etc. attached to something to identify it or give information about it
words.example: He put name tags on all his shirts.

words.def: the part that sticks out at the back of the body of a bird, an animal or a fish, which the animal can move from side to side or up and down
words.example: The dog ran up, wagging its tail.

words.def: a story created using the imagination, especially one that is full of action and adventure
a large container for holding liquid or gas
a device for controlling the flow of water from a pipe into a bath or sink
a result that you try to achieve
a hole that has been made in something by tearing
words.example: This sheet has a tear in it.

words.def: connected with the practical use of machines, methods, etc. in science and industry
words.example: We offer free technical support for those buying our software.

words.def: connected with technology
the years of a person’s life when they are between 13 and 19 years old
words.example: in your teens, She began writing poetry in her teens.

words.def: a building used for religious worship, especially in religions other than Christianity
words.example: the Temple of Diana at Ephesus

words.def: in a way that lasts or is intended to last or be used only for a short time; in a way that is not permanent
words.example: We regret this service is temporarily unavailable.

words.def: lasting or intended to last or be used only for a short time; not permanent
if somebody/something has a particular tendency, they are likely to behave or act in a particular way
a situation in which people do not trust each other, or feel unfriendly towards each other, and that may cause them to attack each other
words.example: There is mounting tension along the border.

words.def: a word or phrase used as the name of something, especially one connected with a particular type of language
the conditions that people offer, demand or accept when they make an agreement, an arrangement or a contract
very
words.example: I'm terribly sorry—did I hurt you?

words.def: to make somebody feel extremely frightened
land that is under the control of a particular country or political leader
a feeling of extreme fear
the use of violent action in order to achieve political aims or to force a government to act
a person who takes part in terrorism
words.example: The terrorists are threatening to blow up the plane.

words.def: the activity of testing somebody/something in order to find something out, see if it works, etc.
a book that teaches a particular subject and that is used especially in schools and colleges
to such a degree; so
words.example: I can't walk that far (= as far as that).

words.def: the crime of stealing something from a person or place
the subject or main idea in a talk, piece of writing or work of art
a specialist who treats a particular type of illness or problem, or who uses a particular type of treatment
the treatment of a physical problem or an illness
words.example: He is receiving therapy for cancer.
